hidden_disagreement.interpretation: FLAG: 5 agents recommended option A, but their underlying reasoning differs substantially (mean RMSE in rating space = 1.46 on a 1-7 scale). Standard ensemble aggregation would report 'strong consensus' here; this is hidden disagreement that may surface as execution conflicts.

low_discrimination_constructs.left_pole: References systemic context of the decision-making environment.
low_discrimination_constructs.right_pole: E2 and E3 are anchored in protocol details and tool specificity, while E1 emphasizes the breakdown of institutional trust.
low_discrimination_constructs.interpretation: All options score near the middle on this axis (References systemic context of the decision-making environment. <-> E2 and E3 are anchored in protocol details and tool specificity, while E1 emphasizes the breakdown of institutional trust.). Either none of the options addresses this dimension, or agents collectively underweighted it. Worth probing explicitly: does any option strongly hit one pole here?

blind_spots.summary: 1 construct(s) where agents agreed all options are middling - candidate blind spots to probe.

risk_surface.note: Risks listed here were extracted by pattern matching on agent free responses. They are minority signals worth probing before acting on consensus.

operator_questions: Agents M1 (neutral), M2 (neutral), M3 (neutral), M4 (neutral), M5 (neutral) agreed on option A - but their reasoning differs (diversity=1.46). Which framing will drive execution? Different framings will produce different execution paths.

All options scored near the middle on the axis 'References systemic context of the decision-making environment.' vs 'E2 and E3 are anchored in protocol details and tool specificity, while E1 emphasizes the breakdown of institutional trust.'. Does any option deserve a strong position here? If not, is this a dimension being underweighted?

Consensus is strong (4-5 agents agree). Is this because the answer is genuinely obvious, or because agents share a common training distribution? If you cannot articulate why the OTHER options were rejected, the consensus may be inherited, not earned.
headline: 5/5 agents recommended option A, but with substantially different reasoning (RMSE=1.46). Reported consensus hides actionable disagreement.
